skill.name: thiserror-expert
skill.description: Provides guidance on creating custom error types with thiserror, including proper derive macros, error messages, and source error chaining. Activates when users define error enums or work with thiserror.
skill.summary: Guides Rust developers in designing idiomatic custom errors with thiserror.
skill.author: EmilLindfors

security_audit.summary: The static external command detections are false positives from markdown code fences and inline Rust examples, not executable shell or Ruby code. The weak cryptography and system reconnaissance detections are also false positives from prose and Rust error-handling examples. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, network access, or executable helper scripts were found in SKILL.md.

content.value_statement: Rust projects often grow complex custom error types with repetitive boilerplate. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward clear thiserror patterns.
actual_capabilities: Suggests thiserror derives for custom Rust error enums.
Explains when to use from, source, and transparent annotations.
Improves error messages that include tuple or named fields.
Designs layered error types for domain and infrastructure code.
Identifies missing source chains in Rust error variants.
limitations: Does not compile or test Rust code by itself.
Does not replace project-specific API design review.
Focuses on thiserror, not every Rust error-handling crate.
Requires source context to make precise recommendations.

use_cases.title: Refactor Manual Error Implementations
use_cases.description: Convert verbose Display and Error implementations into concise thiserror enums.

use_cases.title: Design Library Error APIs
use_cases.description: Create stable public error types with clear variants and useful source chains.

use_cases.title: Review Error Messages
use_cases.description: Check whether error variants expose useful context without leaking implementation details.

prompt_templates.title: Convert Basic Error Enum
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner refactor from manual Rust errors.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Rust error enum and suggest a thiserror version with clear messages.

prompt_templates.title: Add Source Chaining
prompt_templates.prompt: Find missing source chains in these Rust errors and explain whether to use from or source.

prompt_templates.title: Design Layered Errors
prompt_templates.prompt: Design thiserror enums for domain, infrastructure, and application layers in this Rust module.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Public Error API
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these public Rust error types for thiserror usage, API stability, message quality, and source preservation.

output_examples.input: A Rust enum manually implements Display and Error for two variants.
output_examples.output: The skill recommends deriving thiserror::Error, adding clear error messages for each variant, and removing boilerplate trait implementations.

output_examples.input: An error variant wraps an IO error without marking it as a source.
output_examples.output: The skill explains that the source chain is lost and recommends marking the wrapped error as the source.

output_examples.input: A library exposes one broad error variant for every dependency failure.
output_examples.output: The skill suggests more precise variants, transparent forwarding where appropriate, and stable messages for public callers.

best_practices: Provide the relevant Rust error enum and nearby call sites.
State whether the error type is public API or internal implementation.
Ask for message clarity, conversion behavior, and source chaining together.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to hide real source errors behind vague messages.
Do not use transparent forwarding when callers need added context.
Do not request project-wide changes without showing module boundaries.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps design Rust custom error types using the thiserror crate.

faq.question: Does it run code?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides guidance and examples. It does not execute Rust code.

faq.question: Can it replace anyhow?
faq.answer: No. It explains when thiserror is useful, especially for typed errors and library APIs.

faq.question: Will it preserve source errors?
faq.answer: It specifically checks for source chains and explains when to use source, from, or transparent annotations.

faq.question: Is it useful for beginners?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains common patterns and shows how thiserror reduces boilerplate.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: It is listed for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
